ginger.name: ginger

storage.temperature: refrigerated (45-50°F) or room temp for short periods
storage.container: paper bag in crisper, or wrap in paper towel then plastic bag
tips: Unpeeled ginger lasts longer than peeled
Freeze ginger and grate from frozen - easier to handle
Store in dry place if keeping at room temp (up to 1 week)
Look for firm, smooth skin - avoid wrinkled or moldy pieces

antioxidants.gingerol: very high - responsible for pungency and health benefits
antioxidants.shogaol: high in dried ginger - more pungent than gingerol

tips: Freeze ginger and grate from frozen - no need to peel
Younger ginger has thinner skin and milder flavor
Add early in cooking for mellow flavor, late for punch
Crush slices to release more flavor in broths
preparation.yields: 1 inch fresh = 1 tbsp grated = 1/4 tsp ground

ginger.description: Ginger (Zingiber officinale) is a rhizome with a sharp, warming flavor that's both sweet and pungent. Indispensable in Asian cuisines and increasingly popular in Western cooking, it adds depth to both savory and sweet dishes. Fresh ginger is preferred for most applications, while dried ground ginger is traditional in baking.
healthBenefits: May reduce muscle pain and soreness

nutmeg.name: nutmeg

tips: Buy whole nutmeg and grate fresh - incomparably better flavor
Use a microplane or dedicated nutmeg grater
Pre-ground nutmeg loses flavor very quickly

antioxidants.myristicin: high - psychoactive in large doses, aromatic

methods: Add at end of cooking for most aroma
tips: A little goes a long way - very potent
Freshly grated is 10x more aromatic than pre-ground
Toxic in large quantities - use sparingly
Pairs beautifully with dairy and cream

nutmeg.description: Nutmeg is the seed of Myristica fragrans, encased in mace (another spice). It has a warm, sweet, slightly hallucinogenic aroma that's essential in baking, cream sauces, and eggnogs. Widely used in both sweet and savory applications across European, Middle Eastern, and Caribbean cuisines. The complex flavor comes from myristicin and eugenol in its essential oils.
healthBenefits: May aid sleep and reduce insomnia
Digestive aid - reduces gas and bloating

preparationTips: Good for subtle color and flavor
Building flavor for rubs and marinades

storage.notes: Seeds can be sprouted if fresh

techniques.tadka: bloom in hot oil and pour over dish

sauces.name: Sauces
sauces.method: toast and grind or leave whole

preparation.notes: Blend can be adjusted for heat preference

cardamom.name: cardamom

tips: Buy whole pods and grind seeds as needed for maximum flavor
Green cardamom is most common; black is smokier
Pods protect volatile oils - much longer shelf life than ground
Store away from moisture to prevent mold

methods: Grind seeds in spice grinder or mortar
tips: Use only the seeds, discard papery pods
Lightly crush pods before adding to dishes whole
Grind fresh for each use - loses flavor rapidly
A little goes a long way - very aromatic

cardamom.description: Cardamom is the seed pod of a plant in the ginger family, considered the 'Queen of Spices'. Green cardamom is floral and sweet with notes of eucalyptus and mint. Black cardamom is smokier and more robust. Essential in chai, Indian sweets, and Scandinavian baking, cardamom is one of the world's most expensive spices after saffron and vanilla.

saffron.name: saffron

storage.container: airtight, opaque container away from light
tips: Store in dark container - light degrades color and flavor
Keep threads whole until ready to use
Never buy saffron powder - easily adulterated

antioxidants.crocin: very high - provides golden color and antioxidant benefits
antioxidants.safranal: high - distinctive aroma and potential mood benefits

methods: Steep threads in warm liquid 10-20 minutes
Toast lightly and crush before steeping
Add threads directly to rice dishes
Grind with sugar for even distribution
tips: A little goes a long way - use 10-20 threads per serving
Steep in warm water, milk, or stock before adding
Toast threads briefly to intensify flavor
Adds both color and distinctive hay-like, floral flavor

saffron.description: Saffron consists of the dried stigmas of Crocus sativus flowers, hand-harvested and the world's most expensive spice by weight. Each flower yields only 3 stigmas, requiring 75,000 flowers for one pound of saffron. It imparts a distinctive golden color, subtle floral flavor, and hay-like aroma to dishes. Essential in paella, risotto Milanese, and Persian rice.
healthBenefits: May improve mood and reduce depression
May suppress appetite and aid weight loss

chili_powder.name: chili powder

tips: Make your own blend for better flavor control
Store away from heat and light
Replace every 6 months for optimal flavor
Note: chili powder vs. pure ground chili - different products

tips: Chili powder is a blend (chili, cumin, garlic, oregano)
Ground chili is pure dried chili
Blooming in fat releases fat-soluble capsaicin
Add early for integrated heat, late for fresh spice flavor
preparation.yields: Use as needed - typically 1-3 tsp per dish

chili_powder.description: Chili powder is a spice blend typically containing ground dried chiles, cumin, garlic powder, oregano, and sometimes paprika. Different from pure ground chile peppers, it's designed as an all-in-one seasoning for chili con carne and Tex-Mex dishes. Heat levels vary by brand and chile types used.

coriander.name: coriander

tips: Buy whole seeds for better flavor retention
Toast before grinding to enhance citrus notes
Store away from light to preserve essential oils
Seeds should have pleasant citrus aroma, not musty

antioxidants.linalool: very high - floral, citrus aroma compound

tips: Toasting is essential - brings out sweet, nutty notes
Freshly ground coriander is much more aromatic

coriander.description: Coriander seeds come from the same plant as cilantro (Coriandrum sativum), but taste completely different - sweet, citrusy, and warming rather than fresh and herbaceous. Essential in curry powders, pickling spices, and Middle Eastern cuisine. The seeds develop their characteristic flavor when toasted, releasing aromatic linalool.

grinding.notes: Discard pods or use for infusing

Black pepper for most savory applications

black_pepper.description: Black pepper (Piper nigrum) is the world's most traded spice, prized for its sharp, pungent flavor and aromatic complexity. The peppercorns are the dried, unripe fruits of the pepper vine. Its heat comes from piperine, which also enhances the bioavailability of other nutrients. Essential in virtually all savory cooking, pepper is a foundational seasoning in global cuisine.

storage.notes: Keep dry but expects some moisture

synergistic_actions.respiratory_relief: Works well with thyme and elecampane

Once cut, wrap tightly and refrigerate
Remove any sprouting or soft onions immediately

antioxidants.quercetin: very high - especially in red onions, anti-inflammatory
antioxidants.anthocyanins: high in red onions - heart health

methods: Halve from root to stem, peel, leave root intact
Slice pole to pole for even cooking
Dice by making horizontal and vertical cuts
Chill before cutting to reduce tears
tips: Sharp knife reduces cell damage and fewer tears
Sweet onions (Vidalia, Walla Walla) for mild flavor
The longer you cook, the sweeter they become

onion.description: Onions are fundamental aromatics in virtually every world cuisine. From the French mirepoix to the Cajun 'holy trinity,' onions form the flavor foundation of countless dishes. Raw, they're pungent and sharp; cooked slowly, they transform into sweet, caramelized magic. The sulfur compounds that make you cry also provide significant health benefits.

garlic.name: garlic

storage.container: open air basket or mesh bag, not plastic
tips: Store whole bulbs in open air, never refrigerate
Keep away from moisture to prevent mold
Once separated into cloves, use within 10 days
Peeled cloves can be refrigerated in olive oil (use within 1 week)

antioxidants.allicin: very high - forms when garlic is crushed, powerful antimicrobial

methods: Crush with side of knife to remove skin easily
Mince finely for maximum allicin release
Roast whole heads for mild, sweet flavor
tips: Let crushed garlic sit 10 minutes before cooking for maximum health benefits
Remove green germ from center if sprouting (bitter)
Add early for mellow flavor, late for punch
Burnt garlic is bitter - watch carefully when sautéing

garlic.description: Garlic is one of the most important ingredients in global cuisine, used in virtually every savory culinary tradition. A member of the allium family with onions and leeks, garlic transforms dramatically based on preparation: raw it's intensely pungent, sautéed it's mellow and aromatic, roasted it's sweet and creamy. The health benefits are legendary, with allicin providing powerful antimicrobial properties.
healthBenefits: May lower blood pressure and cholesterol

hardneck.characteristics: harder central stem, fewer but larger cloves
hardneck.flavor: complex, often spicier, better for raw applications

storage.notes: Can be regrown in water from roots

preparation.notes: Can be used whole for presentation

storage.notes: Store in plastic bag with moisture

preparation.notes: Soak in cold water to reduce pungency

carrot.name: carrot

tips: Remove green tops before storing - draws moisture from roots
Keep away from apples and pears (ethylene producers)
Baby carrots dry out faster - use within 1-2 weeks

antioxidants.beta_carotene: extremely high - converted to vitamin A, orange pigment

methods: Peel with vegetable peeler (or scrub well if organic)
tips: Young carrots don't need peeling - just scrub
Cut into even sizes for uniform cooking
Cook with fat to increase beta-carotene absorption

carrot.description: Carrots are root vegetables prized for their sweet flavor and vibrant orange color from beta-carotene. While orange is most common, carrots come in purple, yellow, red, and white varieties. A staple in mirepoix and other aromatic bases, carrots are equally delicious raw or cooked. The beta-carotene becomes more bioavailable when cooked with fat.
healthBenefits: Extremely high in beta-carotene - eye health

storage.notes: Store in paper bag in crisper drawer

preparation.notes: Soak in water with lemon juice to prevent browning

asparagus.name: asparagus

storage.container: upright in jar with 1 inch water, covered with plastic bag
tips: Store like flowers - upright in water
Use within 3-4 days for best flavor
Can blanch and freeze for up to 8 months

Peel thick stalks with vegetable peeler
Roast at high heat for caramelization
tips: Bend stalks to find natural breaking point
Thin asparagus cooks faster than thick
Thick asparagus has more flavor, thin more tender
Shave raw into ribbons for salads

asparagus.description: Asparagus is a spring vegetable prized for its delicate flavor and tender texture. The edible shoots range from pencil-thin to thumb-thick, with thicker spears generally having more flavor. Green asparagus is most common, while white asparagus (grown underground) is prized in Europe for its mild, refined flavor. Purple varieties are sweeter but turn green when cooked.
healthBenefits: Rich in folate - important for pregnancy

sauteed.method: Slice or dice, sauté with olive oil until tender-crisp

grilled.method: Slice lengthwise, brush with oil, grill until charred

baked.method: Hollow out and stuff with fillings, bake until tender

spiralized.method: Use spiralizer to create noodle-like strands

antioxidants.lutein_zeaxanthin: very high - eye health, macular degeneration prevention

methods: Remove thick stems from mature leaves
tips: Wash just before use, not before storing
Baby spinach tender enough for stems
Spinach wilts dramatically - 1 lb fresh = 1 cup cooked
Add lemon or vitamin C to improve iron absorption

spinach.description: Spinach is a nutrient-dense leafy green packed with vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. Baby spinach is tender and mild, perfect for salads, while mature spinach has heartier leaves ideal for cooking. Extremely high in vitamin K and a good source of plant-based iron (though less bioavailable than animal sources). Wilts dramatically when cooked, reducing in volume by 90%.
healthBenefits: Extremely high in vitamin K - bone health
Plant-based iron (enhanced absorption with vitamin C)

tips: Can be processed into rice substitute or mashed as potato replacement

romanesco.characteristics: lime green, fractal pattern, nutty flavor

broccoli.name: broccoli

tips: Wrap loosely in damp paper towel
Yellow florets indicate age - use immediately
Can blanch and freeze for up to 12 months

antioxidants.sulforaphane: very high - powerful anti-cancer compound, enhanced by chewing raw
antioxidants.indole_3_carbinol: high - hormone balance and cancer prevention

methods: Blanch in boiling salted water 2-3 minutes
tips: Don't discard stems - peel and slice for cooking
Blanch then shock in ice water for vibrant color
Roast at high heat for caramelized edges
Raw broccoli has more sulforaphane than cooked

broccoli.description: Broccoli is a cruciferous vegetable packed with nutrients and cancer-fighting compounds. The edible flower buds and stems offer a slightly bitter, earthy flavor that becomes nutty and sweet when roasted. Rich in sulforaphane—a potent antioxidant formed when broccoli is chopped or chewed. Both the florets and stems are edible and nutritious.
healthBenefits: High in sulforaphane - powerful anti-cancer properties
Rich in vitamins C and K

tomato.name: tomato

storage.temperature: room temperature until ripe, then refrigerate 45-50°F for up to 1 week
storage.duration: 5-7 days at room temp when ripe, 1-2 weeks refrigerated

tomato.description: Tomatoes are technically fruits but culinarily treated as vegetables. Available in countless varieties from tiny cherry tomatoes to massive beefsteaks, they're fundamental to cuisines worldwide. Rich in lycopene (especially when cooked), tomatoes provide umami depth and bright acidity. Peak season summer tomatoes are incomparably sweet and complex compared to winter greenhouse varieties.
healthBenefits: Rich in lycopene - prostate and heart health
High in vitamin C - immune support

bell_pepper.name: bell pepper

storage.container: crisper drawer in plastic bag with holes
tips: Red, yellow, orange peppers sweeter than green (riper)
Store unwashed until ready to use
Can freeze roasted peppers for up to 6 months

antioxidants.vitamin_c: extremely high - more than oranges per serving

methods: Remove stem, seeds, and white ribs
Roast whole, then peel for silky texture
tips: Green = unripe, red/yellow/orange = ripe and sweeter
Roast over open flame or under broiler for charred flavor
Peel easily after roasting in covered bowl 10 minutes
Raw in salads for maximum vitamin C

bell_pepper.description: Bell peppers are sweet, mild members of the Capsicum family (unlike their spicy cousins). They're actually unripe (green) or ripe (red, yellow, orange) fruits of the same plant. Red peppers are the sweetest as they're fully ripened. Extremely high in vitamin C—one medium red pepper provides 169% of the daily value. Essential in Mediterranean, Asian, and Latin American cuisines.
healthBenefits: Extremely high in vitamin C - immune support

tips: Salt and drain before cooking to remove bitterness

nutritionalProfile.notes: High in beta-carotene and vitamin A
Excellent source of vitamin A and beta-carotene
Good source of vitamin C and potassium

avocado.name: avocado

storage.container: open air until ripe, then refrigerate

Yields to gentle pressure when ripe
Refrigerate ripe avocados to slow further ripening
Brush cut surfaces with lemon juice to prevent browning

tips: Hass avocados best for flavor and creaminess

Slightly underripe for slicing, perfectly ripe for mashing

avocado.description: Avocados are unique fruits prized for their buttery texture and rich, mild flavor. Unlike most fruits, they're high in healthy fats—primarily monounsaturated oleic acid. Native to Mexico, avocados (also called alligator pears) are now cultivated in warm climates worldwide. Hass avocados with pebbly dark skin are most prized for eating. Ripe avocados yield to gentle pressure.
healthBenefits: Rich in potassium - more than bananas

banana.name: banana

storage.duration: 3-7 days at room temp depending on ripeness

tips: Store at room temperature - refrigeration turns skin black

antioxidants.dopamine: moderate - not crossing blood-brain barrier but antioxidant
antioxidants.resistant_starch: high in green bananas - prebiotic benefits

tips: Green bananas: starchy, great for cooking
Yellow bananas: sweet, perfect for eating
Spotted bananas: very sweet, ideal for baking
Black bananas: overripe but perfect for banana bread

banana.description: Bananas are the world's most popular fruit, prized for their convenience, sweetness, and energy-providing carbohydrates. Available year-round, they ripen naturally after harvest, becoming sweeter as starches convert to sugars. The Cavendish variety dominates commercial production. Rich in potassium and vitamin B6, bananas are a perfect portable snack and versatile cooking ingredient.
healthBenefits: Excellent source of potassium - heart and muscle health
High in vitamin B6 - brain health
Provides quick energy from natural sugars

lemon.name: lemon

storage.temperature: room temperature for 1 week, refrigerate for 2-4 weeks

tips: Room temperature lemons yield more juice

antioxidants.limonene: very high - in peel, anti-cancer and mood-enhancing properties
antioxidants.citric_acid: high - provides tartness and preservative qualities
antioxidants.flavonoids: high - especially in peel and pith

methods: Roll firmly on counter before juicing

lemon.description: Lemons are the most versatile citrus fruit, providing bright acidity and aromatic complexity to both savory and sweet dishes. The juice, zest, and even preserved peel are culinary essentials. Rich in vitamin C and flavonoids, lemons have been valued for centuries for both culinary and medicinal purposes. Meyer lemons are sweeter and more fragrant than standard Eureka lemons.
healthBenefits: High in vitamin C - immune support

nutritionalProfile.notes: Rich in monounsaturated fats and antioxidants

storage.notes: Will continue to yellow over time

orange.name: orange

storage.temperature: room temperature for 1 week, refrigerate for 2-3 weeks
storage.duration: 1 week at room temp, 2-3 weeks refrigerated

Zest can be frozen for later use

antioxidants.hesperidin: very high - flavonoid that reduces inflammation

tips: Roll firmly on counter before juicing
Navel oranges for eating, Valencia for juicing
Blood oranges have raspberry notes and burgundy flesh

orange.description: Oranges are the world's most popular citrus fruit, prized for sweet juice and bright flavor. Navel oranges are ideal for eating fresh with their thick, easy-to-peel skin and seedless flesh. Valencia oranges are juicier and preferred for juice. Blood oranges have distinctive burgundy flesh and berry-like notes. Choose firm, heavy oranges with smooth skin.
healthBenefits: Excellent source of vitamin C - immune support

preparation.notes: Pink varieties are sweeter than white
Very easy to eat out of hand

storage.notes: Very large fruit, may need refrigeration

preparation.peeling: thick rind, easy to peel when scored

storage.notes: Highly aromatic and prized in Japanese cuisine
Primarily used for zest and oil in Earl Grey tea

Very easy to peel and segment

blueberry.name: blueberry

Wild blueberries more intense flavor than cultivated

tips: Wild blueberries smaller but more flavorful

blueberry.description: Blueberries are small, round berries with deep blue-purple skin and a silvery bloom. One of the few fruits native to North America, blueberries are prized for their sweet flavor and extraordinary antioxidant content—among the highest of all common fruits. Wild blueberries are smaller and more intensely flavored than cultivated varieties. Fresh blueberries should be firm, plump, and deeply colored.
healthBenefits: Extremely high in antioxidants - among highest of all fruits

Room temperature berries have best flavor

strawberry.description: Strawberries are beloved for their bright red color, juicy texture, and sweet-tart flavor. Unlike most fruits, the seeds are on the outside. Peak season berries are incomparably sweet and aromatic compared to off-season varieties. Extremely high in vitamin C and antioxidants. Choose berries that are uniformly red with fresh green hulls.
origin: Hybrid of North and South American species
healthBenefits: Extremely high in vitamin C - immune support

apple.description: Apples are one of the most widely cultivated fruits, with thousands of varieties ranging from tart to sweet. Crisp and refreshing raw, they transform beautifully when cooked, breaking down into tender sweetness. The saying 'an apple a day keeps the doctor away' reflects their reputation as a healthful food. Choose firm apples with smooth, unblemished skin.
healthBenefits: High in fiber - digestive and heart health

nutritionalProfile.notes: Higher copper content than most fruits
